news.headline: Novo Nordisk's denecimig (Mim8) demonstrated positive results in long-term safety and efficacy in phase 3 hemophilia A FRONTIER extension study at ISTH 2026
news.summary: Interim FRONTIER4 extension data presented at the ISTH 2026 Congress in Paris showed durable safety and efficacy for denecimig prophylaxis across adults, adolescents and children with hemophilia A. The results support Novo's push to diversify its pipeline into rare bleeding disorders alongside the core GLP-1 franchise.

news.headline: Wegovy Strategy Overhaul? Novo Nordisk Korea Restructures Executives and Organization
news.summary: Novo Nordisk Korea merged its obesity and diabetes divisions and installed a local physician-executive to speed up decision-making, after Eli Lilly's Mounjaro overtook Wegovy to hold roughly 78% of the domestic injectable obesity market versus about 15% for Wegovy. The reorganization illustrates how competitive pressure from Lilly is forcing tactical changes to Novo's GLP-1 commercial strategy outside the US.

news.headline: Medicare obesity drug GLP-1 coverage starting July 1
news.summary: Eligible Medicare beneficiaries gained access to Wegovy (injection and pill) for a $50/month copay from July 1, 2026 via the Medicare GLP-1 Bridge program, running through end-2027, with Novo and Eli Lilly supplying CMS at a negotiated net price of $245/month. The program opens a large new reimbursed patient pool ahead of the January 2027 US list-price cut.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk: Wegovy pill (semaglutide tablets) becomes first daily GLP-1 weight-loss pill approved in the UK
news.summary: The UK's MHRA approved the Wegovy pill for weight management, making the UK the first European market and third globally (after the US and UAE) to clear the oral GLP-1. Novo said the pill would become available via private prescription within weeks, extending its oral-obesity franchise ahead of Eli Lilly's international rollout.

news.headline: Novo Nordisk's CagriSema 2.4 mg / 2.4 mg demonstrated significant reduction in HbA1c and weight across multiple studies in the REIMAGINE program presented at ADA 2026
news.summary: Full REIMAGINE 1-3 Phase 3 results presented at the ADA 2026 Scientific Sessions showed CagriSema achieved superior HbA1c reductions (up to about 1.9 percentage points) and weight loss (up to about 14%) versus semaglutide alone in type 2 diabetes. The data, simultaneously published in The Lancet and The Lancet Diabetes & Endocrinology, bolster Novo's case ahead of the FDA's expected Q4 2026 decision on CagriSema for obesity.

news.headline: Wegovy pill prescriptions surpass 3 million, 1 filled roughly every 5 seconds, bringing GLP-1 therapy to people with obesity previously untreated
news.summary: Novo Nordisk said US Wegovy pill prescriptions topped 3 million within just over five months of launch, with more than 80% going to patients new to GLP-1 therapy. The rapid uptake, roughly one script filled every five seconds, signals strong momentum for the oral franchise ahead of Eli Lilly's competing Foundayo pill.

catalysts.label: H1/Q2 2026 earnings release
catalysts.detail: Financial report for the six months to 30 June 2026, due 5 August 2026 (07:30 CEST); focus on Wegovy pill adoption post-UK launch and Medicare Bridge uptake, underlying obesity-care momentum ex one-off pricing, and any guidance revision.

catalysts.label: FDA decision on CagriSema
catalysts.detail: Expected FDA verdict on the first once-weekly GLP-1/amylin combination (semaglutide + cagrilintide) for chronic weight management, filed on REDEFINE 1/2 data; trials showed up to roughly 20-23% weight loss. Key re-rating catalyst.

catalysts.label: Zenagamtide (amycretin) Phase 3 diabetes program start
catalysts.detail: Novo plans to move its next-generation GLP-1/amylin dual agonist zenagamtide (amycretin) into a pivotal Phase 3 program in type 2 diabetes in H2 2026, following Phase 2 data (up to ~14.6% weight loss) presented at ADA 2026. A future obesity Phase 3 start is also expected.

catalysts.label: Higher-dose CagriSema Phase 3 start
catalysts.detail: A higher-dose version of CagriSema is scheduled to enter Phase 3 in the second half of 2026; the larger REDEFINE-11 outcomes study reads out in H1 2027.

catalysts.label: U.S. list-price cut takes effect
catalysts.detail: Wegovy, Ozempic and Rybelsus list prices drop to roughly $675/month (about -50%/-35%), coinciding with new Medicare-negotiated pricing under the GLP-1 Bridge; a margin headwind but a volume-defense move.

pulse: Novo Nordisk's news flow over the past six weeks skews constructive on the US front but shows mounting competitive strain abroad: the Wegovy pill topped 3 million US prescriptions and won UK approval, Medicare's GLP-1 Bridge extended $50-copay access from July 1, and fresh CagriSema REIMAGINE diabetes data reinforced the case ahead of the FDA's expected Q4 2026 obesity decision. At the same time, Novo Nordisk Korea's merger of its obesity and diabetes units underscores how far Eli Lilly's Mounjaro/Zepbound have eroded Wegovy's share in some markets, even as Novo diversifies with promising hemophilia (denecimig) data. The next major checkpoints are the 5 August H1 earnings print and the Q4 2026 CagriSema FDA verdict, set against a scheduled roughly 50% US list-price cut in January 2027.
